summary: Removed a fake 4-byte empty function split in sysdolphin baselib texpdag. The placeholder symbol fn_80386230 and its empty C stub were deleted, and make_full_dependancy_mtx was resized in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from 0x130 to 0x134 so the bytes formerly attributed to the fake function belong to the preceding function.
what_changed: Deleted the empty stub `void fn_80386230(void) {}` from `src/sysdolphin/baselib/texpdag.c`.
Removed the `fn_80386230 = .text:0x80386230; // type:function size:0x4` symbol entry from `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.
Updated `make_full_dependancy_mtx` in `symbols.txt` from size `0x130` to `0x134`, keeping its start address at `.text:0x80386100`.
Left `HSD_TExpSchedule` starting at `.text:0x80386234`, so the deleted fake function's 4 bytes are absorbed by the preceding function rather than moving later symbols.
smart_moves: Fixed the fake function at the symbol-boundary level instead of preserving a meaningless empty C function.
Adjusted only the preceding function size, avoiding churn to the following `HSD_TExpSchedule` address and later texpdag symbols.
Kept the source cleanup minimal: deleting the stub while preserving the surrounding static padding declaration.
decomp_lessons: A 4-byte placeholder `fn_XXXXXXXX` between real functions may just be a mis-split terminator or tail byte of the previous function; verify boundaries before modeling it as a real empty function.
When removing a fake function, update `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` so adjacent function sizes still cover the same address range.
Progress reports can show a removed fake function as a broken/deleted match even when the cleanup improves the neighboring unmatched item; interpret function-count reductions in context.
naming_conventions: Address-derived placeholder name `fn_80386230` was removed rather than promoted to a named function.
Existing project spelling `make_full_dependancy_mtx` was retained exactly; this PR did not attempt a naming or typo cleanup.
Existing HSD-style exported names such as `HSD_TExpMakeDag` and `HSD_TExpSchedule` were left unchanged.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Folded the 4-byte range at `0x80386230` into `make_full_dependancy_mtx` by increasing its symbol size from `0x130` to `0x134`.
Maintained the next function boundary at `HSD_TExpSchedule = .text:0x80386234`.
Used symbol-table correction plus source deletion to eliminate an artificial empty-function match.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev reported total functions `19830 (-1)` and matched code `57.57% (-0.00%, -4 bytes)`.
decomp.dev listed `fn_80386230` as a broken/removed 4-byte match and `make_full_dependancy_mtx` as an unmatched-item improvement from `97.50%` to `98.83%` with `+8` bytes.
follow_up_queries: Search for other 4-byte `fn_XXXXXXXX` placeholders adjacent to unmatched functions that may be fake symbol splits.
Audit texpdag function boundaries around `make_full_dependancy_mtx` and `HSD_TExpSchedule` if future matching changes touch this range.
Check whether other symbol entries can be cleaned up by resizing neighboring functions instead of carrying empty stubs.
